slots.caption_ig:
Scoring a dim sum cart: skin translucency, filling-to-wrapper ratio, sauce freshness, cart rotation speed. The best har gow we've scored: Yin Ji Rice Roll in Chinatown. 97/100. Walk-in.

#ForkFox #DimSum #PhillyFood #FoodScience #DishNotRestaurant
slots.caption_fb: Scoring dim sum is its own discipline. We look at skin translucency (you should almost see the filling), filling-to-wrapper ratio, sauce freshness, and how fast the cart is rotating (cold dim sum is failed dim sum). The best har gow we've scored: Yin Ji Rice Roll in Philly Chinatown. 97/100.
slots.image_gen_prompt: Top-down macro of dim sum baskets — har gow, siu mai, char siu bao. Steam rising. Editorial restaurant lighting. 1080x1080.
slots.video_gen_prompt: Vertical 9:16, 8s. Macro of har gow being picked up with chopsticks. Skin is translucent. Scoring labels animate in: 'Skin: 9.5', 'Filling: 9.3'. End: 'Dim sum, scored.'
